_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Bilateral Orchidectomy for hormone ablation/ : Bilateral Orchidectomy for hormone ablation_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Bilateral Orchidectomy for hormone ablation_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/DNB or Equivalent (in Urology/ Surgical Oncology)
clinical_indications: Cancer of testes, Prostate cancer (Hormone dependent), Trauma or Torsion of testes
Other conditions like Male breast cancer

preauth.label: Clinical Notes confirming diagnosis and need for surgery

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history especially previous surgery, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advise for admission, and planned line of treatment?
Was CT/MRI/ +/- FNAC/Biopsy report indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: CT/MRI/ +/- FNAC/Biopsy report not indicative of surgery, or missing at pre-authorization.
Clinical notes are incomplete or do not clearly confirm diagnosis and need for surgery at pre-authorization.
Detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details are missing at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are missing at claim submission.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Bilateral orchidectomy, or surgical castration, is a surgery to remove both testicles. It is a treatment option for locally advanced and metastatic prostate cancer and aims to stop the production of androgens. Hormonal therapy is the standard treatment for metastatic prostatic carcinoma. The conventional surgical or medical androgen ablation therapy seems to have a similar response.
surgical_options: Simple Orchidectomy: Usually done bilaterally to halt the production of testosterone in cancers.
Subcapsular Orchidectomy: Is performed to treat hormone-dependent prostate cancer. The capsule of the testes is left behind to maintain the Shape of the Scrotum.
Radical orchidectomy: Removal of the testis and the spermatic card.
Eliminating testosterone from the blood stream of a prostate cancer patient will provide a period of disease remission.
